title: Human resources and industrial relations officers
description: Recruit, screen, interview, or place individuals within an organization. May perform other activities in multiple human resources areas. Recruit and hire seasonal or temporary agricultural laborers. May transport, house, and provide meals for workers. Resolve disputes between workers and managers, negotiate collective bargaining agreements, or coordinate grievance procedures to handle employee complaints. (Based on 6 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Direct and transport workers to appropriate work sites, and provide food, drinking water, and sanitation facilities to contracted workers.
Employ foremen to directly manage workers during recruitment, hiring, training, task assignments, and enforcement of work rules.
Supervise and monitor the work of contracted employees to ensure tasks are performed correctly.
Pay wages of contracted farm laborers and verify employment records for accuracy.
Develop methods to monitor employee satisfaction, including grievance or complaint procedures, and advise management on these matters.
Negotiate collective bargaining agreements and present the company's position during labor negotiations.
Review employer practices for compliance with contractual agreements on wages, hours, and working conditions.
Investigate and evaluate union complaints or arguments, and determine their viability.
Counsel employees on personnel policies, benefits, and grievance procedures, ensuring clarity on employment regulations.
Prepare and maintain personnel records related to recruitment, training, grievances, and performance evaluations.
Maintain current knowledge of labor laws, including Equal Employment Opportunity (EEO) and affirmative action guidelines.
Plan and conduct orientations for new employees and develop recruiting strategies to meet staffing needs.
Compile reports regarding employment activities and conduct analysis of employment-related data.
Interview job applicants to verify qualifications and ensure they meet job requirements.
Review employment applications and conduct reference checks to ensure candidate eligibility.
Administer employee benefit programs, including insurance and compensation plans, and ensure compliance with policies.
